title: Envoys signal no breakthrough on bridging Russia and Ukraine’s military, political differences
description: The negotiations in Switzerland were the third round of direct talks organized by the U.S. Expectations for significant progress in Geneva were low.

title: US to send more missile launchers to the Philippines despite China’s alarm
description: The addition of missile launchers comes as treaty allies condemned what they called China’s “illegal, coercive, aggressive, and deceptive activities.”

title: Airbus open to two-fighter option for FCAS to keep program alive
description: “The deadlock of a single pillar should not jeopardize the entire future of this high-tech European capability, which will bolster our collective defense."

title: Robert Mugabe's son charged with attempted murder in South Africa
description: Bellarmine Mugabe and one other man have not commented on the charges after a 23-year-old was shot.

title: In Brussels, volunteers assemble drones to be sent to Ukraine
description: In undisclosed locations, volunteers meet regularly in the Belgian capital to assemble drones destined for Ukraine. Teachers, European civil servants, members of the Ukrainian diaspora and former military personnel are participating in this initiative coordinated by the NGO Wings for Europe. Funded by private donations, these devices are then sent to the front lines. FRANCE 24's Alix Le Bourdon and Dave Keating report from Brussels.

title: Why are israel and india getting closer?
description: India’s Prime Minister Narendra Modi is heading to Israel, where he’s set to meet Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: BAE says its Eurofighter pipeline is filled until first GCAP assembly
description: Europe accounted for 32% of the BAE order book at the end of December, while accounting for 11% of sales in 2025, the company said.

title: Pentagon official blesses Europe’s push to spend defense money at home
description: “Otherwise people in Germany or Poland or whatever are going to say: ‘Why are we only sending money across the Atlantic?’” said the Pentagon policy chief.

title: Pentagon to deploy roughly 200 troops to Nigeria
description: The 200 troops will supplement a small team of American military officers already embedded with Nigerian forces.

title: Experts worry about nuclear quid pro quo in Russia-North Korea alliance against Ukraine
description: Analysts differ on the question of whether Moscow would trade away highly sensitive nuclear submarine technology in exchange for battlefield support.

title: Estonia begins buying hundreds of pop-up bunkers to fortify border with Russia
description: The Baltic nations are installing a variety of border security features meant to repel an invasion from Russia or Belarus.

title: ‘You either fight or die’: Kenyans tricked into joining Russia-Ukraine war
description: More than 1,000 Kenyans and people from 36 African nations are fighting for Russia, many of them recruited fraudulently.

title: In Pakistan, female boxers break down gender barriers to step into the ring
description: In Pakistan, more and more women are stepping into the ring — a space long seen as the preserve of men. At the 5th National Women’s Boxing Championship, participation surpassed 100 athletes for the first time, competing across eight weight divisions — compared to just three at the tournament’s inaugural edition in 2018.

title: More protests in Iran as talks continue with US
description: Students ⁠held protests which led ​to clashes at several Iranian universities for a ​second day on Sunday, according to local news agencies and social media posts, with Iran facing a U.S. military ​buildup ‌as it seeks to reach a ⁠nuclear deal with Washington.

title: New York orders citywide travel ban as major blizzard hits eastern US
description: New York ordered drivers off the road and shut down schools on Monday, while residents braced for a massive snowstorm hitting the United States northeast. Tens of millions of Americans from the US capital, Washington, to the northern state of Maine have prepared for up to two feet (60 centimeters) of snow forecast in some areas.

title: Iran says ready for talks but will defend itself against US aggression
description: Remarks from Ministry of Foreign Affairs come after Trump says he is considering an attack if nuclear deal not reached.

title: Gaza child dies after Israel blocks medical evacuation
description: Palestinian boy Nidal Abu Rabeea died in Gaza after waiting months for Israel to allow his medical evacuation.

title: Gaza child dies waiting for Israeli permission to leave for treatment
description: Israel continues to block thousands of Palestinians from seeking medical treatment abroad through Rafah crossing.

title: Tour Montparnasse Is One of Paris’s Uglier Landmarks. It’s Getting a Makeover.
description: The Tour Montparnasse is one of Paris’s least-loved landmarks. After half a century, it’s finally being remodeled.

title: For Iran’s Rulers, Refusing U.S. Demands Is a Risk Worth Taking
description: The government in Tehran sees capitulating to Washington’s demands on uranium enrichment and ballistic missiles as riskier to its survival than going to war, analysts say.

title: EU Support for Ukraine Stumbles as Hungary Looks to Delay Aid
description: Hungary said that it would block both the latest sanctions package on Russia and a financial aid package to Kyiv worth about $106 billion.

title: The Ukrainian Bureaucrat Working to Squeeze Russia’s War Machine
description: Vladyslav Vlasiuk has spent the past four years pressing Western allies to squeeze the Russian economy through more sanctions. He hopes that 2026 will be the tipping point.

title: Scoop: Hegseth to meet Anthropic CEO as Pentagon threatens banishment
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has summoned Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to the Pentagon on Tuesday morning for what sources say is likely to be a tense meeting over terms for military use of Anthropic's Claude."Anthropic knows this is not a get-to-know-you meeting," a senior Defense official told Axios. "This is not a friendly meeting. This is a sh*t-or-get-off-the-pot meeting."Why it matters: Claude is the only AI model available in the military'

title: New partnerships enhance Canada’s readiness for future naval projects
description:
Ontario Shipyards and Hanwha Ocean have formalised an MoU aimed at advancing Canada’s capacity to undertake future naval projects.
The post New partnerships enhance Canada’s readiness for future naval projects appeared first on Naval Technology.

title: Massive US Air Force warplane movements in Bulgaria raise stakes for Iran talks
description: The U.S. buildup coincides with high-stakes nuclear diplomacy over Iran's nuclear program.

title: US calls on partners to stick to existing trade deals amid tariff uncertainty
description: After a weekend of trade upheaval in the wake of the US Supreme Court's decision to invalidate US president's tariffs that invoked the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, and Donald Trump's subsequent decision to apply 15 percent global tariffs based on another law, we look at the latest developments in global trade. Also in this edition, robotaxis are vowing to hit the streets of London by the end of 2026.

title: Russia's 'new secret agents' wage a war of sabotage in Europe
description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, February 23, 2026: First, ahead of the four-year anniversary of the war in Ukraine, papers discuss Russia's war plans, and their new agents. Next, the Guardian takes a closer look at "El Mencho". Also, as the Olympics come to an end, Olympians are celebrated in papers around the world. Finally, the final candidates for European Tree of the year have been named!

title: Gazans face impossible wait at Rafah crossing to Egypt
description: Photos have circulated showing celebrations as formerly exiled Gazans have been able to return home since the partial reopening of the Rafah border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt. However, that isn’t the story for the majority of people seeking to cross: with only a few dozen people allowed through each day, many Gazans are stuck waiting, with no sense of when or if they will be able to cross.

title: 'Love doesn't die with death': How four years of war have reshaped relationships in Ukraine
description: As Ukraine prepares to mark the fourth anniversary of Russia’s brutal invasion, FRANCE 24 spoke to Maryna Kumeda, the Ukrainian author of “Love in Wartime”, about how the war is reshaping attitudes to love and relationships.

title: 'Joy amid the destruction': Gazans decorate rubble-strewn streets for Ramadan
description: Little Ramadan lanterns and string lights have appeared on streets lined with collapsed buildings and piles of rubble in Gaza City, bringing a small moment of joy and respite as Islam's holiest month began - the first since October's ceasefire - gets underway.

title: Iran-US set for last-ditch talks as strike fears build
description: Iranian officials held out hope for progress towards a deal to forestall fresh conflict when talks with US negotiators resume on Thursday, despite a huge build-up of American military might in the Middle East. France 24's Kethevane Gorjestani takes a look at whether an agreement seems likely, and what might happen if one cannot be reached.

title: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani orders citywide shutdown as snowstorm hits
description: The citys entire traffic network is shut down except for emergency travel as a severe snowstorm hits northeast US.

title: Ramadan in Gaza: Cost of iftar doubles as genocidal war devastates economy
description: Al Jazeera analysis shows cost of a basic Ramadan meal in Gaza doubles under Israeli siege.

title: Russia kills four Ukrainians on eve of fourth war anniversary
description: EU foreign ministers are meeting in Brussels to discuss a loan for Kyiv and 20th package of sanctions against Russia.

title: More than 200 Venezuelan political prisoners launch hunger strike over amnesty law exclusions
description: More than 200 Venezuelan political prisoners began a hunger strike on Sunday, demanding inclusion in a new amnesty law they say excludes many detainees, particularly those facing military-related charges. The law, approved by Congress last week, forms part of reforms backed by Washington after former president Nicolas Maduro’s January ouster.

title: Premier League: Arsenal pass derby test of nerve against Tottenham
description: Eberechi Eze and Victor Gyökeres allowed Arsenal to restore their lead at the top of the Premier League table to five points with victory in the North London derby (4-1).

title: Live: Several killed in Ukraine's Odesa as strikes intensify across Ukraine
description: Russian strikes killed three people and wounded several others overnight, regional authorities said Monday, as Moscow intensified its assaults on the eve of the war's four-year anniversary. In the south, two people were killed when Russian drones hit industrial, energy and civilian infrastructure in the Odesa region, regional governor Oleg Kiper said on Telegram.Follow our live blog for all the latest devleopments.

title: Pakistan claims at least 70 fighters killed in strikes along Afghan border
description: Afghan officials accuse Pakistan of targeting civilians and violating its sovereignty in Sundays border air raids.

title: The Ukraine war in numbers: People, territory, money
description: Four years into Russias grinding war on Ukraine, Al Jazeera looks at the costs — to people, territory and treasuries.

title: Four years into its full-scale war in Ukraine, Russia is feeling the effects
description: Steve Rosenberg reports on the economic consequences of Russia's war, and how people are coping.

title: Duterte’s Fans Rally in The Hague as the I.C.C. Weighs Evidence Against Him
description: The protests are a sign of the Philippine ex-leader’s ongoing influence, even as he faces a possible trial for the deaths of thousands during his war on drugs.

title: Mexico killed "El Mencho." Here's how and what we know about U.S. role in the operation
description: Mexican security forces killed Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, the country's most-wanted drug lord, following information from the U.S. that assisted in Sunday's operation, the Trump administration and Mexico's defense ministry said.Why it matters: President Trump has for months pressed Mexican officials to target drug cartels and the U.S. offered a reward of up to $15 million for information leading to the arrest of Oseguera Cervantes, known a

title: China calls on US to cancel Trump-era tariffs following Supreme Court ruling
description: China on Monday urged the United States to cancel unilateral tariffs announced by President Donald Trump after the US Supreme Court struck down many of his measures, ruling he lacked authority under a 1977 law. Trump responded by unveiling new global import duties of up to 15% under different powers.

title: Paris court to review Nicolas Sarkozy's request to merge sentences in graft case
description: A Paris criminal court will on Monday review former president Nicolas Sarkozy’s request to combine sentences handed down in separate convictions for corruption and illegal campaign financing. Sarkozy, who served from 2007 to 2012, has faced multiple legal cases since leaving office. His lawyer said such requests are routine.

title: How Modi ‘broke down walls’ between India, Israel – at Palestine’s expense
description: Under Modi, India, a long supporter of the Palestinian cause, has found a friend in Israels Netanyahu despite genocide.

title: Russia-Ukraine war: List of key events, day 1,460
description: These are the key developments from day 1,460 of Russia’s war on Ukraine.

title: Tour Montparnasse is One of Paris’s Uglier Landmarks. It’s Getting a Makeover.
description: The Tour Montparnasse is one of Paris’s least-loved landmarks. After half a century, it’s finally being remodeled.
